# Technical Specification: How We Work Section — Modern Landing Page Redesign

## 1. Overview

Adds a 4-step process timeline section to `LandingPage.razor`. On desktop the steps are arranged horizontally with a CSS connector line; on mobile they stack vertically. Step numbers display the accent gradient. Content is declared statically in the component (not JSON-driven).

**Key business value:** Communicating the engagement process proactively removes uncertainty and reduces sales friction before a prospect makes first contact.

**Scope and constraints:**
- Static content in markup — no new data file; process steps are a presentation concern owned by design
- Connector line must be pure CSS (no image assets)
- Must integrate with the `reveal-item` stagger animation system introduced in the Hero Section story

---

## 2. Data / Content Layer

### 2.1 Content Source

The four steps are **static markup** in `LandingPage.razor`. No JSON file is required.

Step content (based on the consultancy context from `site-properties.json` and `services.json`):

| Step | Title | Description |
|------|-------|-------------|
| 1 | Discovery | We learn your business goals, constraints, and priorities in a focused first session. |
| 2 | Solution Design | We propose an architecture and delivery approach tailored to your context. |
| 3 | Build & Iterate | We deliver working software in short cycles, adjusting as you give feedback. |
| 4 | Handover & Support | We document, transfer knowledge, and remain available for ongoing support. |

> **Trade-off:** Static copy is simpler to implement and keeps the component self-contained. If marketing or content teams need to edit the steps without a developer, extract to a `process-steps.json` in `wwwroot/data/` following the same pattern as `services.json`.

---

## 3. UI Component

### 3.1 File Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `Components/Pages/LandingPage.razor` | Add `How We Work` section markup with `id="how-we-work"` |
| `Components/Pages/LandingPage.razor.css` | Add timeline grid, connector, step number gradient, and responsive rules |

### 3.2 Markup Structure

```razor
<section id="how-we-work" class="process-section" aria-labelledby="process-heading">
    <div class="section-inner">
        <h2 id="process-heading" class="section-title reveal-item" style="--reveal-delay: 0ms">
            How We Work
        </h2>

        <ol class="process-steps" role="list">
            <li class="process-step reveal-item" style="--reveal-delay: 80ms">
                <div class="step-number" aria-hidden="true">01</div>
                <h3 class="step-title">Discovery</h3>
                <p class="step-description">
                    We learn your business goals, constraints, and priorities in a focused first session.
                </p>
            </li>
            <li class="process-step reveal-item" style="--reveal-delay: 160ms">
                <div class="step-number" aria-hidden="true">02</div>
                <h3 class="step-title">Solution Design</h3>
                <p class="step-description">
                    We propose an architecture and delivery approach tailored to your context.
                </p>
            </li>
            <li class="process-step reveal-item" style="--reveal-delay: 240ms">
                <div class="step-number" aria-hidden="true">03</div>
                <h3 class="step-title">Build &amp; Iterate</h3>
                <p class="step-description">
                    We deliver working software in short cycles, adjusting as you give feedback.
                </p>
            </li>
            <li class="process-step reveal-item" style="--reveal-delay: 320ms">
                <div class="step-number" aria-hidden="true">04</div>
                <h3 class="step-title">Handover &amp; Support</h3>
                <p class="step-description">
                    We document, transfer knowledge, and remain available for ongoing support.
                </p>
            </li>
        </ol>
    </div>
</section>
```

> **Accessibility note:** The `<ol>` uses `role="list"` to restore list semantics in Safari when `list-style: none` is applied via CSS. Step numbers are wrapped in `aria-hidden="true"` div elements because the semantic order of the `<ol>` already communicates sequence; the visual numbers are decorative.

### 3.3 CSS — Process Timeline (`LandingPage.razor.css`)

```css
.process-section {
    padding: 5rem 2rem;
    background: var(--color-surface-base);
}

/* Desktop: 4-column horizontal grid with connector */
.process-steps {
    display: grid;
    grid-template-columns: repeat(4, 1fr);
    gap: 0;
    list-style: none;
    padding: 0;
    margin: 0;
    position: relative;
}

/* Connector line — spans the step number row, sits behind the numbers */
.process-steps::before {
    content: '';
    position: absolute;
    top: 1.75rem;                   /* vertically centred with the step-number circle */
    left: calc(12.5% + 1.75rem);   /* starts after the first step number */
    right: calc(12.5% + 1.75rem);  /* ends before the last step number */
    height: 2px;
    background: var(--color-accent-gradient);
    opacity: 0.35;
    z-index: 0;
}

.process-step {
    display: flex;
    flex-direction: column;
    align-items: center;
    text-align: center;
    padding: 0 1rem;
    position: relative;
    z-index: 1;
}

/* Step number circle with accent gradient */
.step-number {
    width: 3.5rem;
    height: 3.5rem;
    border-radius: 50%;
    background: var(--color-accent-gradient);
    color: var(--color-text-inverse);
    font-size: 1rem;
    font-weight: 700;
    display: flex;
    align-items: center;
    justify-content: center;
    margin-bottom: 1.25rem;
    flex-shrink: 0;
    box-shadow: var(--shadow-xs);
}

.step-title {
    font-size: 1.05rem;
    font-weight: 600;
    color: var(--color-text-primary);
    margin: 0 0 0.5rem;
}

.step-description {
    font-size: 0.9rem;
    color: var(--color-text-secondary);
    line-height: 1.6;
    margin: 0;
}

/* Tablet: 2×2 grid, hide connector */
@media (max-width: 1023px) {
    .process-steps {
        grid-template-columns: repeat(2, 1fr);
        gap: 2rem;
    }

    .process-steps::before {
        display: none;
    }

    .process-step {
        padding: 0;
    }
}

/* Mobile: single column, hide connector */
@media (max-width: 767px) {
    .process-steps {
        grid-template-columns: 1fr;
        gap: 2.5rem;
    }

    .process-step {
        align-items: flex-start;
        text-align: left;
        flex-direction: row;
        gap: 1rem;
    }

    .step-number {
        margin-bottom: 0;
        flex-shrink: 0;
    }
}
```

---

## 4. Testing Requirements

### UI Tests (Playwright)

| Test | Action | Expected |
|------|--------|----------|
| Section renders | Navigate to `/` | `<section id="how-we-work">` exists in DOM |
| Exactly 4 steps | Inspect list | `ol.process-steps` has exactly 4 `li` children |
| Steps numbered 01–04 | Inspect `.step-number` elements | Text content: `01`, `02`, `03`, `04` in order |
| Section heading present | Inspect DOM | `<h2 id="process-heading">How We Work</h2>` (or equivalent) |
| Desktop connector visible | Viewport ≥1024px | `process-steps::before` pseudo-element has height > 0 |
| Mobile no overflow | Viewport 375px | No horizontal scrollbar; steps stack in one column |
| Step numbers are decorative | Inspect DOM | Each `.step-number` has `aria-hidden="true"` |
| `<ol>` structure | Inspect DOM | Steps wrapped in `<ol>` not `<div>` |
| Stagger animation classes | Inspect DOM | Each `li` has `reveal-item` class and `--reveal-delay` style set |

### Reduced-Motion Test

| Test | Action | Expected |
|------|--------|----------|
| All steps visible immediately | `emulateMedia({ reducedMotion: 'reduce' })` | All 4 `.process-step` elements have `opacity: 1` with no transition |

---

## 5. Implementation Notes

### Architectural Decisions

- **Static markup over JSON:** Process steps describe internal methodology, not service offerings. They change rarely and are typically ownership of design/engineering rather than content teams. Static markup reduces implementation complexity without loss of maintainability.
- **CSS `::before` pseudo-element for connector:** Avoids SVG complexity. The connector is positioned absolutely between the first and last step numbers using percentage-derived `left`/`right` values. This avoids layout dependency between steps.
- **`<ol>` with CSS grid layout:** The ordered list provides semantic sequence while CSS grid provides the visual horizontal layout. This contrasts with a `<div>` grid, which would require explicit ARIA to convey order.
- **Step numbers hidden from accessibility tree:** The `<ol>` already conveys ordering semantics. Adding `aria-hidden="true"` to the number element prevents screen readers from announcing "01 Discovery" (redundant "01") — instead they hear "item 1 of 4, Discovery".

### Dependencies & Build Order

1. Confirm `reveal-item` CSS and `IntersectionObserver` JS are in place (Hero Section story)
2. Add process section markup to `LandingPage.razor` — after Services section, before Technologies
3. Add process section CSS to `LandingPage.razor.css`

### Edge Cases & Error Handling

- If content is ever moved to JSON: use the same `Http.GetFromJsonAsync<ProcessStep[]>` pattern; component markup change is minimal (replace static `<li>` blocks with `@foreach`)
- On extra-narrow viewports (320px): `clamp()` on font sizes may need applying to `.step-description` if text overflows

### Performance Considerations

- No network requests for this section (static content)
- `::before` connector uses `background: var(--color-accent-gradient)` — a CSS gradient, not an image — zero network cost

---

## Prerequisites

- [Hero Section Spec](./hero-section-spec.md) — CSS tokens, `.reveal-item` system, and `section-inner`/`section-title` shared styles must be in place
- [Services Section Spec](./services-section-spec.md) — `id="services"` anchor must exist for logical page flow; How We Work follows Services in DOM order
